candidates.candidate: Keep the complete body track primary and make the companion page subordinate.
candidates.decision_reason: This is the core reader-package behavior under test.

candidates.candidate: Read the book as a diagnosis shift from painful effort to energy-system design.
candidates.decision_reason: Strong explanatory value, but must be phrased as interpretation.

candidates.candidate: Energy sources expand action options: play, control, and connection.
candidates.decision_reason: Useful to the reader, but not unique enough to dominate the companion page.

candidates.candidate: Treat stuck work as an action-design problem before treating it as a character flaw.
candidates.decision_reason: This is both source-supported and actionable.

candidates.candidate: Use the opening crisis as proof that high-pressure responsibility is always bad.
candidates.decision_reason: The text critiques a failed productivity diagnosis, not responsibility itself.

candidates.candidate: Rest and calibration are system maintenance.
candidates.decision_reason: Useful but familiar; keep as support rather than headline.

candidates.candidate: Happy-productivity absolutism: if it feels good, it must be productive.
candidates.decision_reason: This overclaims and would mislead readers.
